<!-- GENERATED by github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/docgen. DO NOT EDIT. -->
# Package reference

One page per internal package that carries a package-level doc comment, generated by `golc.ps1 docs` (internal/docgen).

- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/artnet](./artnet.md) — Package artnet implements GOLC's Art-Net output layer (ARTN-01..ARTN-06, CONTEXT D-03/D-04): a standalone daemon (daemon.go) owns the Worker, fan-out target map, and pinned network interface for the lifetime of the process and serves every CLI or UI client over the named-pipe transport in internal/artnet/ipc -- no client ever constructs its own Worker or output process.
- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/artnet/ipc](./artnet-ipc.md) — Package ipc implements the named-pipe transport connecting the Art-Net daemon (internal/artnet) to its CLI and UI clients (04-04/04-05-PLAN.md): Dial and Forward on the client side, a Listener and dispatch loop on the daemon side.
- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/bootstrap](./bootstrap.md) — Package bootstrap implements the checksum-controlled installation boundary for pinned project-local tools.
- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/command](./command.md) — Package command implements the GOLC route/scope self-registration contract (CONTEXT D-03): each command file declares its exact route with MustDeclareRoute and each owning command graph declares its scope with MustDeclareScope from package-level var initializers.
- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/contracts](./contracts.md) — Package contracts is GOLC's single deterministic JSON Schema generation and drift-check registry (CONTEXT D-08): the authoritative Go type behind every config concern, the fixture definition, and each Linear contract self-registers a SchemaDescriptor through MustRegisterSchema from a package-level var initializer, mirroring internal/command's own MustDeclareRoute/MustDeclareScope self-registration idiom.
- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/delivery](./delivery.md) — Package delivery is the single declarative owner of GOLC's offline core command graph (CONTEXT D-02/D-03/D-10): the fixed generate/check/build/ test execution order, each step's network policy, and the offline environment/deny-transport contract every network-denied step runs under.
- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/deployment](./deployment.md) — Package deployment models concrete fixture deployments (CONTEXT POOL-02/D-09): a Deployment is a named, saved mapping of logical pool members to concrete fixture Instances (mode, universe, address), with exactly one Deployment marked Active at a time.
- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/docgen](./docgen.md) — Package docgen extracts each internal package's godoc-style package comment and renders it as a standalone Markdown reference page.
- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/fixture](./fixture.md) — Package fixture is GOLC's canonical, capability-based fixture domain model (CONTEXT D-08): GDTF-friendly rather than hard-wired to Open Fixture Library's channel/mode shape, with every Capability.Range normalized to the 0..1 range so the model stays protocol-agnostic (Art- Net today, other protocols later).
- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/fixture/ofl](./fixture-ofl.md) — Package ofl implements the Open Fixture Library import path (FIXT-03/FIXT-06, CONTEXT D-06/D-08): fetch.go is an SSRF-guarded (threat T-02-06), cache-backed client that validates a target URL's scheme and host before issuing a fixed-timeout, size-capped GET, and normalize.go maps the fetched definition's channel/capability shape onto GOLC's canonical fixture.FixtureDefinition through the exact same validation and pinning pipeline the hand-authored YAML path uses.
- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/playback](./playback.md) — Package playback implements GOLC's real-time deterministic playback engine (CONTEXT SCEN-06/SCEN-09, D-05..D-08): Engine publishes its active frame and plan exclusively through atomic.Pointer, so every UI/persistence/scripts/API/LLM adapter crosses the tick loop boundary with a non-blocking Load/Store and can never block or backpressure live output.
- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/pool](./pool.md) — Package pool models fixture pools and groups (CONTEXT POOL-01/D-10) and their reviewable impact-plan machinery (POOL-03/D-11): a Pool is a named logical grouping of compatible fixtures defined independently of concrete member count, address, or deployment hardware, and a Group is an independent cross-pool named selection -- both with durable UUIDv7 identity that is never derived from or re-minted by a display name.
- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/programming](./programming.md) — Package programming is GOLC's attribute programmer (PROG-02/PROG-03): ProgrammerState is the in-memory scratch buffer of every semantic intensity, color, position, or beam value a show author has touched since the last Clear, always normalized to [0,1] against fixture.Capability.Range rather than a second, parallel bounds concept.
- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/projectconfig](./projectconfig.md) — Package projectconfig loads the committed GOLC root configuration index (golc.project.toml) and its concern files with strict validation and repository containment (CONTEXT D-05).
- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/scene](./scene.md) — Package scene declares GOLC's Scene/Layer domain model (CONTEXT SCEN-01/SCEN-04/SCEN-05): a Scene loops for a configured number of musical bars against the global BPM and combines four independently enabled, independently selectable layers -- base-look, color-theme, chase, motion -- resolved by a fixed priority order.
- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/security](./security.md) — Package security is the single centralized owner of GOLC's secret-safe diagnostic and canary-scan contract (CONTEXT D-19/D-20, T-01-18): every caller that needs to render a sensitive declaration's presence, redact a value before it can be logged, or prove that no fake-secret byte leaked into stdout, stderr, a generated schema, a committed map, a report, a manifest, or a ZIP archive goes through this package rather than re-implementing its own ad hoc masking.
- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/show](./show.md) — Package show is GOLC's durable ShowState substrate (CONTEXT POOL-01/POOL-02/D-16): the working document every Pool, Deployment, Group, and programming.ProgrammerState scratch buffer lives inside, revisioned so a stale impact plan can be detected against a moved Revision.
- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/strictjson](./strictjson.md) — Package strictjson implements the duplicate-safe strict JSON guard (CONTEXT threat T-01-24): a document is accepted only if it is exactly one JSON value and no object anywhere in it repeats a member name.
- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/substitution](./substitution.md) — Package substitution implements semantic fixture substitution (CONTEXT POOL-06/POOL-07/POOL-08, D-14/D-16): BuildSubstitutionPlan diffs two canonical fixture.FixtureDefinition capability sets by CapabilityType -- never by raw DMX channel position -- and reuses pool.BuildImpactPlan's dependent walk plus pool's proven integrity, freshness, and atomic-apply gates verbatim rather than a second mechanism.
- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/trace/apply](./trace-apply.md) — Package apply implements the D-17/D-18/D-21 exact-plan apply contract: a preview built by internal/trace/reconcile is consumed byte-exact, rejected outright if it was tampered with or if relevant repository or Linear state changed since it was produced, and otherwise executed one operation at a time with a strict read-before-write, one-mutation, readback discipline so an uncertain remote outcome (a timeout, a partial GraphQL error, a rate limit) is discovered rather than blindly retried into a duplicate.
- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/trace/catalog](./trace-catalog.md) — Package catalog implements the repository-owned planning identity catalog (CONTEXT D-11/D-12/D-14): durable offline local IDs for the project, active milestone, phases, requirements, dynamically discovered plans, and executable tasks, together with the typed authority split between repository artifacts and Linear operational fields.
- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/trace/reconcile](./trace-reconcile.md) — Package reconcile defines the D-17 exact reconciliation preview contract: repository-derived intent, the credential-free remote mapping set from .planning/linear-map.json, the exhausted current remote observation scope, canonical hash-bound plans, dependency-ordered operations, D-13 field-level conflicts, and the visible, parser-stable D-14 local-ID identity footer.
- [github.com/lnorton89/golc/internal/trace/transport](./trace-transport.md) — Package transport defines the transport-neutral complete-snapshot and mutation contract every Linear adapter implements (CONTEXT D-21; RESEARCH.md Pattern 7).
